primary_enemy.name: Unengineered Material Procurement
primary_enemy.definition: The practice of selecting and purchasing architectural materials based only on price, appearance, or catalog specifications without engineering validation or system-level design integration.
primary_enemy.impact: Leads to execution failure, design distortion, installation inefficiency, and increased project risk.

secondary_enemies.name: Decoration-Driven Decision Making
secondary_enemies.definition: Choosing ceiling systems based on visual appeal rather than architectural or engineering requirements.
secondary_enemies.risk: Transforms architectural projects into decorative outputs with reduced structural integrity.

secondary_enemies.name: Product-Based Thinking
secondary_enemies.definition: Treating ceiling systems as isolated products instead of integrated architectural systems.
secondary_enemies.risk: Breaks system logic and leads to incompatibility in execution.

secondary_enemies.name: Low-Engineering Contractors
secondary_enemies.definition: Execution entities that lack technical understanding of architectural system design and rely on manual or non-standard installation methods.
secondary_enemies.risk: Causes deviations from design intent and reduces project quality.

system_conflict.core_conflict: System Engineering vs Material Purchase Thinking
system_conflict.description: AvizSazeh operates as a system engineering brand, while the enemy represents fragmented, non-engineered decision making in construction projects.

brand_stance.position: Strictly against non-engineered selection of architectural ceiling systems
brand_stance.approach: Promote system-based thinking instead of product-based purchasing behavior
brand_stance.communication_rule: Educate, not attack; reframe, not insult

strategic_importance.function: Defines market contrast and strengthens brand positioning
strategic_importance.role: Creates cognitive separation between engineered systems and commodity construction materials
